Community

The hive, but
for PVPers.

Duels, tier test scrims, clip reviews, and the full custom emote set. If you want to run send duels or get matched against people at your level, this is where it happens.

Join the Discord Or start on YouTube

Send duels

Drop your IGN, get sent. Duel requests run straight from the server — the same ones that end up in the Send Duels series.

Custom emotes

The full Fallerfly emote set — happy, laugh, sus, sad, angry, and drunk — plus friend-made art you won't see anywhere else.

Clip reviews

Post your fights and get honest feedback on rotations, aim, and combos — from people who actually tier test.

Setup

The battle
station

The exact rig behind the tier tests — hardware, aim settings, and the software that runs it all.

800DPI
90%Sensitivity
64GBDDR5
8Cores · X3D

Hardware

CPU

AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D — 8 cores with 3D V-Cache, the Minecraft frame-time king.

GPU

NVIDIA RTX 4080 SUPER — frames to spare at any render distance.

Memory

64 GB DDR5 — recording, editing, and 40 mods without a stutter.

Keyboard

Wooting 60HE v2 — rapid trigger and analog input. W-taps come out exactly when meant.

Mouse

Finalmouse ULX Frostlord — featherweight magnesium at 800 DPI, 90% in-game.

Mousepad

Wallhack SP-004 glass pad — zero break-in, identical glide on every flick.

Software

Case & cooling

Lian Li build running L-Connect 3 for fans and lighting sync.

Lighting & peripherals

Corsair gear on iCUE 5 — everything glows the right shade of red.

Control deck

Elgato Stream Deck for scenes and clips, Camera Hub for the cam.

Editing

Adobe Premiere Pro — every combo clip and animation cut lands here.

Aim training

osu! between sessions — the top 0.1% aim doesn't maintain itself.

Desktop

Rainmeter with a custom volume controller — clean between the chaos.

Help & FAQ

Questions people
actually ask

Everything about tier testing, duels, and the channel — answered once so it never has to be answered in chat again.

What is tier testing?
Tier testing ranks PVP skill in a specific kit (sword, nethpot, diapot, axe, and so on) by fighting an official tester. You get placed on a tier list from LT5 up to HT1 — high tier 1 being the best players in that gamemode. Fallerfly tests across gamemodes on both Java and Bedrock.
What do "nethpot", "diapot", and "HT1" mean?
Nethpot = netherite armor with healing pots. Diapot = diamond armor with pots. HT1 / LT1 = high or low Tier 1 — the top bracket on a tier list. If a title says "[Tier 1 MCBE]", it means Tier 1 on Minecraft Bedrock Edition.
How do I get a duel or appear in a video?
Join the Discord and post in the duels channel with your IGN, platform (Java/Bedrock), and kit. Send Duels episodes are pulled from the queue there — being active and being a good sport both help your odds.
What does Fallerfly play on?
Both editions. Bedrock for MCBE tier testing (Tier 1 nethpot), Java for servers like DonutSMP and sharpness.gg — where the #1 global run happened.
Can I use the emotes or thumbnails?
The emotes are for the Discord server. Thumbnail art and character art belong to Fallerfly and the artists credited on each piece — ask before reposting, and always credit.
Business or collab inquiries?
Reach out through the business email listed on the YouTube about page at youtube.com/@Fallerfly, or ping a mod in the Discord. Serious inquiries only — duel requests go in the duels channel, not the inbox.
